prompt: A SentinelSafe EHS platform capable of running entirely on a disconnected corporate network supports health and safety incident reporting, investigation, and operational analytics for multi-site organizations. Employees and contractors (Reporters) use a Vue.js web interface to sign in with a local username and password, then submit an incident in under two minutes by selecting an incident type (e.g., near miss, injury, property damage, environmental spill), entering a short description, choosing a facility location from a preloaded site list (no maps), confirming the auto-captured time, and optionally attaching up to 5 images (each max 10 MB). Dispatchers triage new reports through a tiered workflow with clear status feedback (New, Acknowledged, In Progress, Escalated, Closed), assign collaborators, and log actions and evidence so the full handling chain is traceable end-to-end. Safety Managers configure incident types, severity rules, and SLAs (default: acknowledge within 15 minutes during business hours and close within 72 hours unless escalated), with in-app reminders appearing on the dashboard when targets are at risk. Users can search incidents and related safety resources with keyword search plus synonym and pinyin matching to support multilingual names, then narrow results using advanced filters such as date range, site, status, risk tags, price (internal chargeback cost for remediation items), rating (internal usefulness score for resources), theme (e.g., PPE, electrical, slips/trips), and origin/destination (for drill routes or transport-related incidents), with sorting by popularity, recent activity, rating, and cost. Administrators and Auditors access visual dashboards for incidents, moderation actions, user activity, and custom reports, with CSV export to a local workstation. The backend uses Express (TypeScript) to expose REST-style APIs consumed by the Vue.js frontend, and persists all data locally in MySQL with strict validation and role-based access control (Administrator, Reporter, Dispatcher, Safety Manager, Auditor). Authentication supports server-side sessions or JWTs; anti-replay protections require short token TTLs (15 minutes), request timestamps (plus/minus 5 minutes), and per-request nonces for state-changing actions. Privilege-escalation checks are enforced on every protected route and written to immutable audit logs capturing who, what, when, and before/after values for data changes. Sensitive fields (phone numbers, IDs, medical notes) are minimized by default, masked in the UI (e.g., showing only last 4 digits), encrypted at rest using AES-256, and transmitted over HTTPS with secure headers plus XSS/CSRF defenses. File uploads are restricted to approved image types, validated by content signatures and size limits, and rejected if metadata exceeds limits or extensions do not match detected formats. Governance features include privacy consent tracking, content moderation using local rules (blocked-term lists, pattern checks for PII leakage), and anti-injection/anti-scraping controls with rate limiting (e.g., 60 requests/minute/user, 10 failed logins per 5 minutes triggers a temporary lockout). Backup and recovery run on a scheduled job that creates encrypted nightly backups retained for 30 days, plus monthly archives retained for 5 years; anomaly alerts are generated locally for unusual events such as mass exports, repeated authorization failures, or sudden spikes in incident edits.
